ops.title: Dedupe is folded-state equality at create time — scan-before-create, never a persisted hash index
ops.body: Create's duplicate guard compares the candidate pack's FOLDED state against the current folded state of live entities of the same kind (scanDup): identity, provenance, and lifecycle fields are ignored (id, nonce, author, timestamps, verify/stale/supersede/tombstone state, task status/assignee/heartbeat/comments/commits, chain head), log Entries are ignored, flag-order permutations of tags/anchors still match, and the oldest live entity wins a tie (List orders by CreatedAt then id). Tombstoned/superseded twins and closed tasks, sprints, and projects are excluded by the live-set queries; stale (expired) notes and docs stay in the live set but the scan skips them, so re-asserting an expired fact roots a fresh entity rather than reviving the stale twin. The scan runs only when dedupeCovered admits the pack shape: a create pack bundling any op that folds into an ignored field (FUSE's NewLog bundles AppendEntry) skips dedupe entirely, so reusing an existing entity can never silently drop one of the pack's ops. A hit surfaces as store.DuplicateError (errors.As) carrying the surviving snapshot: the CLI absorbs it in createEntity (pinned stderr warning + reuse, exit 0), the notes client absorbs it silently (create is idempotent over content). Do not replace the scan with a persisted hash index (refs, in-chain ops, commit trailers, or the git index): a create-time hash checks the wrong thing — every edit/close/tombstone/stale-mark/sync mutates folded state, so a correct index needs distributed invalidation across peers — and adding anything to create commits relocates entity ids (id = create-commit SHA). The guard is best-effort by design: concurrent creates can still race, accepted (scan-before-create over locking).
